figures.caption: Figure 3: Electronic density of states (DOS) of (a) pure Zn using a 2×2×2 supercell and (b) pure Al modeled with its unit cell crystal structure. For both of them, the Fermi level is set to zero.
Figure 5: X-Ray Diffraction (XRD) pattern for pure Zn and 10 wt.% Al into Zn alloy (Zn0.9Al0.1) electrodes.
Figure 2: Schematic illustration of the synthesis process for Zn-Al alloy electrodes.

Figure 9: (a) Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) and (b) Chronoamperometry analyses for pure Zn and Zn-Al alloy-based electrodes in 1 M KOH solution and at 25 ◦C temperature. The applied voltage (Vapp) for chronoamperometry was +0.8 V vs. saturated calomel electrode (SCE).
Table 1: Formation energy (E f ), cohesive energy (Ecoh), work function (ϕ), and Bader charge analyses of the designed Zn-Al alloy electrodes with varying Al contents.
Figure 7: (a) Cyclic voltammetry (CV) and (b) polarization curve (PC) for Zn-Al alloy electrodes in 1 M KOH solution and at 25 ◦C temperature.
Table 3: Anodic Tafel slope (ba), anodic overpotential loss (η0,a), and charge-transfer resistance (RCT) for different electrodes.

Figure 4: Electronic density of states (DOS) of (a) 5% Al into Zn, (b) 10% Al into Zn, (c) 15% Al into Zn, and (d) 20% Al into Zn electrodes using 2×2×2 supercell crystal structure. For all of them, the Fermi level is set to zero.
Figure 8: (a) Anodic Tafel slope and (b) anodic overpotential losses (η0,a) at a current density (J) of 12 mAcm−2 for Zn-Al alloy electrodes in 1 M KOH solution and at 25 ◦C temperature.

Table 2: Anodic Tafel slope (ba), corrosion potential (Ecorr), charge transfer coefficient (αn,a), anodic exchange current density (J0,a), polarization resistance (Rp) and charge-transfer resistance (RCT) for Zn-Al alloy electrodes in 1 M KOH solution and at 25 ◦C temperature.
Figure 1: Hexagonal crystal structure of (a) pure Zn and (b) Zn-Al alloy electrodes.

Figure 6: Optical Microscopy and scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images for (a),(f) Pure Zn, (b),(g) 5 wt.% Al into Zn, (c),(h) 10 wt.% Al into Zn, (d),(i) 15 wt.% Al into Zn, (e),(j) 20 wt.% Al into Zn, respectively.
